content.full_text:
TOWN OF ATHERTON
CALIFORNIA

Administrative Offices
91 ASHFIELD ROAD
ATHERTON, CALIFORNIA 94027-3896
(415) 325-4457
FAX (415) 688-6528

November 19, 1991

The Honorable John A. Fisher
74 Alejandra Avenue
Atherton, California  94027

Dear John:

     This letter responds to yours of October 4, 1991, concerning the possible application of the California Political Reform Act ("Act") to the action of the Atherton Civic Interest League ("League") in sending its September 24, 1991 letter to all Town residents informing the recipients of the League's opposition to proposed Ordinance 466.

     I have checked with our City Attorney on this matter.  He advises me that the Town has no enforcement authority concerning any possible violation of the Act in the manner described.  He has said that if there is concern about a possible violation of the Act, the proper investigating authorities would be either the San Mateo County District Attorney's office in Redwood City, or the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento.

     Please let me know if you need further information.

Sincerely yours,

Christopher E. Cobey
Mayor

cc:  Robin Faisant, Esq.
     Councilmembers
     Richard L. Moore

JOHN A. FISHER
74 ALEJANDRA AVENUE
ATHERTON, CA 94027

October 4, 1991


Hon. Christopher Cobey, Esq.
Town of Atherton
91 Ashfield Rd.
Atherton, CA 94027

RE: Atherton Civic Interest League Lobbying Effort:
Filing Requirement Under State of California Law

Dear Chris:

I was recently chagrined to receive a September 24, 1991 letter from the Atherton Civic
Interest League recommending public opposition to Ordinance #466 of the Town of
Atherton. This letter was addressed to all Atherton residents. I am sure you received a
copy.

This is a first in my experience with the Atherton Civic Interest League. It appears to
constitute a lobbying activity directly affecting an ordinance of the Town. As a consequence,
I am compelled to raise the question of whether the Civic Interest League should be a filing
committee under the Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act and
whether they may be compelled to file California Form 420 and otherwise to establish
themselves as a lobbying organization.

I would recommend that you obtain advice of Town Counsel and of appropriate Sacramento
staff in order to advise the Civic Interest League.

I certainly regret their involvement in an active lobbying campaign. It seems inconsistent
with the historical charter and scope of the Civic Interest League in the Town, but under the
circumstances, I think the question of their status under the Political Reform Act should be
properly addressed.

Sincerely,

John A. Fisher
JAF/n
cc: Robin Faisant, Esq.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Atherton Civic Interest League's September 24, 1991 letter to town residents opposing a proposed ordinance constitutes lobbying activity requiring registration and filing under California's Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is incoming correspondence to a local official, not an FPPC advice letter. The document includes both an initial citizen request (Fisher to Mayor Cobey) and a non-FPPC response (Mayor Cobey's letter) referring the matter to FPPC. No formal FPPC response is present. The Mayor's letter indicates this should be directed to FPPC in Sacramento for proper investigation.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether the Atherton Civic Interest League's September 24, 1991 letter to town residents opposing a proposed ordinance constitutes lobbying activity requiring registration and filing under California's Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: This document contains a citizen's October 4, 1991 letter to the Town Mayor asking whether a civic organization's lobbying effort opposing a local ordinance triggers campaign disclosure and lobbying registration requirements, followed by the Mayor's November 19, 1991 response redirecting the inquiry to the San Mateo County District Attorney or FPPC as the proper investigating authorities.
